title: Revolut Payments Australia Pty Ltd — Regulatory action
investigation_objective: AUSTRAC issued Revolut Payments Australia Pty Ltd a $187,800 infringement notice after Revolut self-disclosed that it had submitted 10 International Funds Transfer Instruction reports late, in breach of the AML/CTF Act's requirement to report such instructions within 10 working days. AUSTRAC's CEO noted that reporting failures carry regulatory consequences even where a company self-detects, discloses and remediates the issue; Revolut cooperated and paid the notice in full.
scope: Paid a $187,800 AUSTRAC infringement notice for late mandatory AML/CTF reporting; Breach was self-disclosed by the company; payment of an infringement notice is not an admission of liability
